1.17.3 3.1.0 (2025 Sep 22)

We are delighted to share the latest 3.1.0 update for XGBoost.

Categorical Re-coder

This release features a major update to categorical data support by introducing a re-coder. This re-coder saves categories in the trained model and re-codes the data during inference, to keep the categorical encoding consistent. Aside from primitive types like integers, it also supports string-based categories. The implementation works with all supported Python DataFrame implementations. (#11609(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11609), #11665(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11665), #11605(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11605), #11628(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11628), #11598(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11598), #11591(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11591), #11568(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11568), #11561(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11561), #11650(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11650), #11621(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11621), #11611(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11611), #11313(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11313), #11311(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11311), #11310(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11310), #11315(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11315), #11303(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11303), #11612(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11612), #11098(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11098), #11347(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11347)) See Auto-recoding(#page=52) (Data Consistency)(#page=52) for more information. (#11297(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11297))
In addition, categorical support for Polars data frames is now available (#11565(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11565)).
Lastly, we removed the experimental tag for categorical feature support in this release. (#11690(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11690)) 
External Memory

We continue the work on external memory support on 3.1. In this release, XGBoost features an adaptive cache for CUDA external memory. The improved cache can split the data between CPU memory and GPU memory according to the underlying hardware and data size. (#11556(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11556), #11465(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11465), #11664(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11664), #11594(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11594), #11469(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11469), #11547(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11547), #11339(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11339), #11477(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11477), #11453(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11453), Also, there’s an optional support (opt-in) for using nvcomp and the GB200 decompression engine to handle sparse data (requires nvcomp as a plugin) (#11451(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11451), #11464(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11464), #11460(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11460), #11512(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11512), #11520(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11520)). We improved the memory usage of quantile sketching with external memory (#11641(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11641)) and optimized the predictor for training (#11548(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11548)). To help ensure the training 


--- PAGE 480 ---
performance, the latest XGBoost features detection for NUMA (Non-Uniform Memory Access) node (      #11538(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11538)  , #11576(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11576) )
for checking cross-socket data access. We are working on additional tooling to enhance NUMA node performance.
Aside from features, we have also added various documentation improvements. (   #11412(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11412)  , #11631(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11631) )
Lastly, external memory support with text file input has been removed (#11562(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11562)). Moving forward, we will focus on iterator inputs.

Multi-Target/Class Intercept

Starting with 3.1, the base-score (intercept) is estimated and stored as a vector when the model has multiple outputs, be it multi-target regression or multi-class classification. This change enhances the initial estimation for multi-output models and will be the starting point for future work on vector-leaf. (#11277(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11277), #11651(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11651), #11625(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11625), #11649(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11649), #11630(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11630), #11647(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11647), #11656(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11656), #11663(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11663))

Features

    • Support leaf prediction with QDM on CPU. (#11620(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11620))
    • Improve seed with mean sampling for the first iteration. (#11639(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11639))
    • Optionally include git hash in CMake build. (#11587(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11587))

Removing Deprecated Features

This version removes some deprecated features, notably, the binary IO format, along with features deprecated in 2.0.
    • Binary serialization format has been removed in 3.1. The format has been formally deprecated in 1.6(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/7547). (#11307(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11307),
      #11553(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11553), #11552(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11552), #11602(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11602))
    • Removed  old  GPU-related  parameters  including  use_gpu  (pyspark),  gpu_id,  gpu_hist,  and
     gpu_coord_descent. These parameters have been deprecated in 2.0. Use the device parameter instead.
      (#11395(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11395), #11554(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11554), #11549(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11549), #11543(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11543), #11539(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11539), #11402(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11402))    • Remove deprecated C functions: XGDMatrixCreateFromCSREx, XGDMatrixCreateFromCSCEx. (#11514(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11514),

      #11513(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11513))
    • XGBoost starts emit warning for text inputs. (#11590(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11590))

Optimizations

    • Optimize CPU inference with Array-Based Tree Traversal (#11519(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11519))
    • Specialize for GPU dense histogram. (#11443(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11443))
    • [sycl] Improve L1 cache locality for histogram building. (#11555(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11555))
    • [sycl] Reduce predictor memory consumption and improve L2 locality (#11603(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11603))

Fixes

    • Fix static linking C++ libraries on macOS (#11522(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11522))
    • Rename param.hh/cc to hist_param.hh/cc to fix xcode build (#11378(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11378))
    • [sycl] Fix build with updated compiler (#11618(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11618))
    • [sycl] Various fixes for fp32-only devices. (#11527(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11527), #11524(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11524))

1.17.7 3.0.0 (2025 Feb 27) 
3.0.0 is a milestone for XGBoost. This note will summarize some general changes and then list package-specific updates. The bump in the major version is for a reworked R package along with a significant update to the JVM packages.


```
      • External Memory Support(#page=483)
      • Networking(#page=483)
      • SYCL(#page=484)
      • Features(#page=484)
      • Optimization(#page=485)
      • Breaking Changes(#page=485)
      • Bug Fixes(#page=485)
      • Documentation(#page=485)
      • Python Package(#page=486)
      • R Package(#page=487)
      • JVM Packages(#page=487)
      • Maintenance(#page=488)
      • CI(#page=488)
```


--- PAGE 483 ---
External Memory Support

This release features a major update to the external memory implementation with improved performance, a new ExtMemQuantileDMatrix(#page=162) for more efficient data initialization, new feature coverage including categorical data support and quantile regression support. Additionally, GPU-based external memory is reworked to support using CPU memory as a data cache. Last but not least, we worked on distributed training using external memory along with the spark package’s initial support.
    • A new ExtMemQuantileDMatrix(#page=162) class for fast data initialization with the hist tree method. The new class
      supports both CPU and GPU training. (#10689(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10689), #10682(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10682), #10886(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10886), #10860(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10860), #10762(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10762), #10694(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10694), #10876(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10876))     • External memory now supports distributed training (  #10492(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10492) , #10861(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10861) ). In addition, the Spark package can use
      external memory (the host memory) when the device is GPU. The default package on maven doesn’t support
      RMM yet. For better performance, one needs to compile XGBoost from the source for now. (#11186(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11186) , #11238(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11238) ,
      #11219(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11219)  )
    • Improved performance with new optimizations for both the     hist -specific training and the approx  (DMatrix(#page=149)  )
      method. ( #10529(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10529) , #10980(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10980) , #10342(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10342) )
   • New demos and documents for external memory, including distributed training. (#11234(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11234), #10929(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10929), #10916(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10916),

      #10426(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10426), #11113(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11113))
    • Reduced binary cache size and memory allocation overhead by not writing the cut matrix. (#10444(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10444))
    • More feature coverage, including categorical data and all objective functions, including quantile regression. In
      addition, various prediction types like SHAP values are supported. (#10918(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10918), #10820(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10820), #10751(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10751), #10724(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10724)) Significant updates for the GPU-based external memory training implementation. (#10924(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10924), #10895(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10895), #10766(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10766), #10544(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10544), #10677(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10677), #10615(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10615), #10927(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10927), #10608(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10608), #10711(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10711))
    • GPU-based external memory supports both batch-based and sampling-based training. Before the 3.0 release,
      XGBoost concatenates the data during training and stores the cache on disk. In 3.0, XGBoost can now stage the      data on the host and fetch them by batch. (#10602(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10602), #10595(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10595), #10606(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10606), #10549(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10549), #10488(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10488), #10766(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10766), #10765(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10765), #10764(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10764),      #10760(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10760), #10753(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10753), #10734(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10734), #10691(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10691), #10713(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10713), #10826(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10826), #10811(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10811), #10810(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10810), #10736(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10736), #10538(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10538), #11333(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11333))     • XGBoost can now utilize NVLink-C2C for GPU-based external memory training and can handle up to terabytes

       of data.
    • Support prediction cache (#10707(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10707)).
    • Automatic page concatenation for improved GPU utilization (#10887(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10887)).
    • Improved quantile sketching algorithm for batch-based inputs. See the section for new features(#page=484) for more info.
    • Optimization for nearly-dense input, see the section for optimization(#page=485) for more info.
See our latest document for details Using XGBoost External Memory Version(#page=78). The PyPI package (pip install) doesn’t have RMM support, which is required by the GPU external memory implementation. To experiment, you can compile XGBoost from source or wait for the RAPIDS conda package to be available.

Networking

Continuing the work from the previous release, we updated the network module to improve reliability. (#10453(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10453), #10756(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10756), #11111(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11111), #10914(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10914), #10828(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10828), #10735(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10735), #10693(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10693), #10676(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10676), #10349(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10349), #10397(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10397), #10566(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10566), #10526(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10526), #10349(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10349)) The timeout option is now supported for NCCL using the NCCL asynchronous mode (#10850(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10850), #10934(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10934), #10945(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10945), In addition, a new  Config(#page=282)   class is added for users to specify various options including timeout, tracker port, etc
for distributed training. Both the Dask interface and the PySpark interface support the new configuration. (#11003(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11003) ,



--- PAGE 484 ---
SYCL

Continuing the work on the SYCL integration, there are significant improvements in the feature coverage for this release from more training parameters and more objectives to distributed training, along with various optimization (#10884(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10884), #10883(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10883)).
Starting with 3.0, the SYCL-plugin is close to feature-complete, users can start working on SYCL devices for in-core training and inference. Newly introduced features include:
    • Dask support for distributed training (#10812(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10812))
    • Various training procedures, including split evaluation (#10605(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10605), #10636(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10636)), grow policy (#10690(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10690), #10681(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10681)), cached
      prediction (#10701(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10701)).
    • Updates for objective functions. (#11029(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11029), #10931(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10931), #11016(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11016), #10993(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10993), #11064(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11064), #10325(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10325))
    • On-going work for float32-only devices. (#10702(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10702))
Other related PRs (#10842(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10842), #10543(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10543), #10806(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10806), #10943(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10943), #10987(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10987), #10548(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10548), #10922(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10922), #10898(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10898), #10576(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10576)) 
Features

This section describes new features in the XGBoost core. For language-specific features, please visit corresponding sections.
    • A new initialization method for objectives that are derived from GLM. The new method is based on the mean
      value of the input labels. The new method changes the result of the estimated base_score. (#10298(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10298), #11331(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11331))     • The xgboost.QuantileDMatrix(#page=155) can be used with all prediction types for both CPU and GPU.
    • In prior releases, XGBoost makes a copy for the booster to release memory held by internal tree methods. We
      formalize the procedure into a new booster method reset()(#page=174) / XGBoosterReset()(#page=433). (#11042(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11042))     • OpenMP thread setting is exposed to the XGBoost global configuration. Users can use it to workaround hard-
      coded OpenMP environment variables. (#11175(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11175))
    • We improved learning to rank tasks for better hyper-parameter configuration and for distributed training.

         – In 3.0, all three distributed interfaces, including Dask, Spark, and PySpark, support sorting the data based
           on query ID. The option for the DaskXGBRanker is true by default and can be opted out. (#11146(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11146), #11007(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11007),            #11047(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11047), #11012(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11012), #10823(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10823), #11023(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11023))

         – Also for learning to rank, a new parameter lambdarank_score_normalization is introduced to make

            one of the normalizations optional. (#11272(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11272))

          – The  lambdarank_normalization        now uses the number of pairs when normalizing the    mean  pair strategy. Previously, the gradient was used for both topk  and mean . #11322(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11322)
    • We have improved GPU quantile sketching to reduce memory usage. The improvement helps the construction
     of the QuantileDMatrix(#page=155) and the new ExtMemQuantileDMatrix(#page=162).

         – A new multi-level sketching algorithm is employed to reduce the overall memory usage with batched

             inputs.

         – In addition to algorithmic changes, internal memory usage estimation and the quantile container is also

            updated. (#10761(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10761), #10843(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10843))

        – The change introduces two more parameters for the QuantileDMatrix(#page=155) and DataIter(#page=176), namely,
          max_quantile_batches and min_cache_page_bytes.
    • More work is needed to improve the support of categorical features. This release supports plotting trees with
      stat for categorical nodes (#11053(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11053)). In addition, some preparation work is ongoing for auto re-coding categories.
      (#11094(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11094), #11114(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11114), #11089(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11089)) These are feature enhancements instead of blocking issues.
    • Implement weight-based feature importance for vector-leaf. (#10700(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10700))



--- PAGE 485 ---
    • Reduced logging in the DMatrix construction. (#11080(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11080))

Optimization

In addition to the external memory and quantile sketching improvements, we have a number of optimizations and performance fixes.
    • GPU tree methods now use significantly less memory for both dense inputs and near-dense inputs. (#10821(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10821),
      #10870(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10870))
    • For near-dense inputs, GPU training is much faster for both hist (about 2x) and approx.
    • Quantile regression on CPU now can handle imbalance trees much more efficiently. (#11275(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11275))
    • Small optimization for DMatrix construction to reduce latency. Also, C users can now reuse the ProxyDMatrix(#page=429)
      for multiple inference calls. (#11273(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11273))
    • CPU prediction performance for QuantileDMatrix(#page=155) has been improved (#11139(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11139)) and now is on par with normal
     DMatrix.
    • Fixed a performance issue for running inference using CPU with extremely sparse QuantileDMatrix(#page=155) (#11250(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11250)).
    • Optimize CPU training memory allocation for improved performance. (#11112(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11112))
    • Improved RMM (rapids memory manager) integration. Now, with the help of   config_context()(#page=146)   , all memory
      allocated by XGBoost should be routed to RMM. As a bonus, all    thrust  algorithms now use async policy.
      (#10873(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10873)  , #11173(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11173) , #10712(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10712) , #10712(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10712) , #10562(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10562) )
    • When used without RMM, XGBoost is more careful with its use of caching allocator to avoid holding too much
      device memory. (#10582(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10582))

Breaking Changes

This section lists breaking changes that affect all packages.
    • Remove the deprecated DeviceQuantileDMatrix. (#10974(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10974), #10491(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10491))
    • Support for saving the model in the deprecated    has been removed. Users can still load old models in 3.0.
      (#10490(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10490)  )
    • Support for the legacy (blocking) CUDA stream is removed (#10607(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10607))
    • XGBoost now requires CUDA 12.0 or later.

Bug Fixes

    • Fix the quantile error metric (pinball loss) with multiple quantiles. (#11279(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11279))
    • Fix potential access error when running prediction in multi-thread environment. (#11167(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11167))
    • Check the correct dump format for the gblinear. (#10831(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10831))

Documentation

    • A new tutorial for advanced usage with custom objective functions. (#10283(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10283), #10725(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10725))
    • The new online document site now shows documents for all packages including Python, R, and JVM-based
    • Lots of enhancements. (#10822(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10822), 11137, #11138(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11138), #11246(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11246), #11266(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11266), #11253(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11253), #10731(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10731), #11222(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11222), #10551(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10551), #10533(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10533))
    • Add a brief description for using the offset from the GLM setting (like Poisson). (#10996(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10996))

    • Cleanup document for building from source. (#11145(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11145))
    • Various fixes. (#10412(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10412), #10405(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10405), #10353(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10353), #10464(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10464), #10587(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10587), #10350(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10350), #11131(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11131), #10815(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10815))

Python Package

    • The feature_weights parameter in the sklearn interface is now defined as a scikit-learn parameter. (#9506(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/9506))
    • Initial support for polars, categorical feature is not yet supported. (#11126(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11126), #11172(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11172), #11116(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11116))
    • Reduce pandas dataframe overhead and overhead for various imports. (#11058(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11058), #11068(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11068))
    • Better xlabel in plot_importance()(#page=244) (#11009(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11009))
    • Validate reference dataset for training. The train()(#page=179) function now throws an error if a QuantileDMatrix(#page=155) is
      used as a validation dataset without a reference. (#11105(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11105))     • Fix misleading errors when feature names are missing during inference (#10814(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10814))
    • Add Stacklevel to Python warning callback. The change helps improve the error message for the Python package.
      (#10977(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10977))
    • Remove circular reference in DataIter. It helps reduce memory usage. (#11177(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11177))
    • Add checks for invalid inputs for cv. (#11255(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11255))
    • Update Python project classifiers. (#10381(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10381), #11028(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11028))
    • Support doc link for the sklearn module. Users can now find links to documents in a jupyter notebook. (#10287(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10287))
   • Dask

         – Prevent the training from hanging due to aborted workers. (#10985(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10985)) This helps Dask XGBoost be robust

             against error. When a worker is killed, the training will fail with an exception instead of hang.

         – Optional support for client-side logging. (#10942(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10942))
         – Fix LTR with empty partition and NCCL error. (#11152(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11152))

         – See the Features(#page=484) section for changes to ranking models.
         – See the Networking(#page=483) section for changes with the communication module.

         – Expose Training and Validation Metrics. (#11133(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11133))
         – Add barrier before initializing the communicator. (#10938(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10938))
         – Extend support for columnar input to CPU (GPU-only previously). (#11299(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11299))

(#11071(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11071), #11211(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11211), #10837(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10837), #10754(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10754), #10347(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10347), #10678(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10678), #11002(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11002), #10692(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10692), #11006(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11006), #10972(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10972), #10907(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10907),

--- PAGE 487 ---
         – Remove dask from the default import. (#10935(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10935)) Users are now required to import the XGBoost Dask

            through:


```
 from xgboost import dask as dxgb
```


             instead of:


```
 import xgboost as xgb
 xgb.dask
```


             The change helps avoid introducing dask into the default import set.

         – Bump Python requirement to 3.10. (#10434(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10434))
         – Drop support for datatable. (#11070(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11070))

R Package

We have been reworking the R package for a few releases now. In 3.0, we will start publishing a new R package on R-universe, before moving toward a CRAN update. The new package features a much more ergonomic interface, which is also more idiomatic to R speakers. In addition, a range of new features are introduced to the package. To name a few, the new package includes categorical feature support, QuantileDMatrix, and an initial implementation of the external memory training. To test the new package:


```
 install.packages('xgboost', repos = c('https://dmlc.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-
 ˓→ project.org'))
```

Also, we finally have an online documentation site for the R package featuring both vignettes and API references (#11166(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11166), #11257(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11257)). A good starting point for the new interface is the new xgboost() function. We won’t list all the feature gains here, as there are too many! Please visit the XGBoost R Package(#page=376) for more info. There’s a migration guide (#11197(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11197)) there if you use a previous XGBoost R package version.
    • Support for the MSVC build was dropped due to incompatibility with R headers. (#10355(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10355), #11150(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11150))
    • Maintenance (#11259(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11259))
    • Related PRs. (#11171(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11171), #11231(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11231), #11223(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11223), #11073(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11073), #11224(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11224), #11076(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11076), #11084(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11084), #11081(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11081), #11072(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11072), #11170(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11170), #11123(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11123),

JVM Packages

The XGBoost 3.0 release features a significant update to the JVM packages, and in particular, the Spark package. There are breaking changes in packaging and some parameters. Please visit the migration guide(#page=412) for related changes. The work brings new features and a more unified feature set between CPU and GPU implementation. (#10639(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10639), #10833(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10833), #10845(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10845), #10847(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10847), #10635(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10635), #10630(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10630), #11179(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11179), #11184(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11184))
    • Automatic partitioning for distributed learning to rank. See the features(#page=484) section above (#11023(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/11023)).
    • Support missing value when constructing dmatrix with iterator (#10628(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/pull/10628))

The 2.1.4 patch release incorporates the following fixes on top of the 2.1.3 release:
    • XGBoost is now compatible with scikit-learn 1.6 (#11021, #11162)
    • Build wheels with CUDA 12.8 and enable Blackwell support (#11187, #11202)
    • Adapt to RMM 25.02 logger changes (#11153)

    • [pyspark] Support large model size (#10984).
    • Fix rng for the column sampler (#10998).
    • Handle cudf.pandas proxy objects properly (#11014).

    • Fix ellpack categorical feature with missing values. (#10906)

1.17.11 2.1.1 Patch Release (2024 Jul 31) 
The 2.1.1 patch release makes the following bug fixes:
    • [Dask] Disable broadcast in the scatter call so that predict function won’t hang (#10632)
    • [Dask] Handle empty partitions correctly (#10559)
    • Fix federated learning for the encrypted GRPC backend (#10503)
    • Fix a race condition in column splitter (#10572)
    • Gracefully handle cases where system files like /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.max are not readable by the user (#10623)
    • Fix build and C++ tests for FreeBSD (#10480)
    • Clarify the requirement Pandas 1.2+ (#10476)
    • More robust endianness detection in R package build (#10642)
In addition, it contains several enhancements:
    • Publish JVM packages targeting Linux ARM64 (#10487)
    • Publish a CPU-only wheel under name xgboost-cpu (#10603)
    • Support building with CUDA Toolkit 12.5 and latest CCCL (#10624, #10633, #10574)

1.17.12 2.1.0 (2024 Jun 20) 
We are thrilled to announce the XGBoost 2.1 release. This note will start by summarizing some general changes and then highlighting specific package updates. As we are working on a new R interface(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/9810), this release will not include the R package. We’ll update the R package as soon as it’s ready. Stay tuned!

Networking Improvements 
An important ongoing work for XGBoost, which we’ve been collaborating on, is to support resilience for improved scaling and federated learning on various platforms. The existing networking library in XGBoost, adopted from the RABIT project, can no longer meet the feature demand. We’ve revamped the RABIT module in this release to pave the way for future development. The choice of using an in-house version instead of an existing library is due to the active development status with frequent new feature requests like loading extra plugins for federated learning. The new implementation features:
    • Both CPU and GPU communication (based on NCCL).
    • A reusable tracker for both the Python package and JVM packages. With the new release, the JVM packages no
      longer require Python as a runtime dependency.
    • Supports federated communication patterns for both CPU and GPU.
    • Supports timeout. The high-level interface parameter is currently hard-coded to 30 minutes, which we plan to
      improve.
    • Supports significantly more data types.
    • Supports thread-based workers.
    • Improved handling for worker errors, including better error messages when one of the peers dies during training.
    • Work with IPv6. Currently, this is only supported by the dask interface.
    • Built-in support for various operations like broadcast, allgatherV, allreduce, etc.
Related PRs (#9597, #9576, #9523, #9524, #9593, #9596, #9661, #10319, #10152, #10125, #10332, #10306, #10208, #10203, #10199, #9784, #9777, #9773, #9772, #9759, #9745, #9695, #9738, #9732, #9726, #9688, #9681, #9679, #9659, #9650, #9644, #9649, #9917, #9990, #10313, #10315, #10112, #9531, #10075, #9805, #10198, #10414).
The existing option of using MPI in RABIT is removed in the release. (#9525) 


--- PAGE 491 ---
NCCL is now fetched from PyPI

In the previous version, XGBoost statically linked NCCL, which significantly increased the binary size and led to hitting the PyPI repository limit. With the new release, we have made a significant improvement. The new release can now dynamically load NCCL from an external source, reducing the binary size. For the PyPI package, the nvidia-nccl-cu12 package will be fetched during installation. With more downstream packages reusing NCCL, we expect the user environments to be slimmer in the future as well. (#9796, #9804, #10447) 
Parts of the Python package now require glibc 2.28+ 
Starting from 2.1.0, XGBoost Python package will be distributed in two variants:
    • manylinux_2_28: for recent Linux distros with glibc 2.28 or newer. This variant comes with all features en-
      abled.
    • manylinux2014: for old Linux distros with glibc older than 2.28. This variant does not support GPU algorithms
      or federated learning.
The pip package manager will automatically choose the correct variant depending on your system.

Starting from May 31, 2025, we will stop distributing the manylinux2014 variant and exclusively distribute the manylinux_2_28 variant. We made this decision so that our CI/CD pipeline won’t have depend on software components that reached end-of-life (such as CentOS 7). We strongly encourage everyone to migrate to recent Linux distros in order to use future versions of XGBoost.
Note. If you want to use GPU algorithms or federated learning on an older Linux distro, you have two alternatives:
   1. Upgrade to a recent Linux distro with glibc 2.28+. OR
   2. Build XGBoost from the source.

Multi-output

We continue the work on multi-target and vector leaf in this release:
    • Revise the support for custom objectives with a new API, XGBoosterTrainOneIter. This new function sup-
      ports strided matrices and CUDA inputs. In addition, custom objectives now return the correct shape for prediction. (#9508)     • The hinge objective now supports multi-target regression (#9850)
    • Fix the gain calculation with vector leaf (#9978)
    • Support graphviz plot for multi-target tree. (#10093)
    • Fix multi-output with alternating strategies. (#9933)
Please note that the feature is still in progress and not suitable for production use.

Federated Learning

Progress has been made on federated learning with improved support for column-split, including the following updates:
    • Column split work for both CPU and GPU. In addition, categorical data is now compatible with column split.
      (#9562, #9609, #9611, #9628, #9539, #9578, #9685, #9623, #9613, #9511, #9384, #9595)     • The use of UBJson to serialize split entries for column split has been implemented, aiding vector-leaf with
    • Documentation and small fixes. (#9610, #9552, #9614, #9867)



--- PAGE 492 ---
Ongoing work for SYCL support 
XGBoost is developing a SYCL plugin for SYCL devices, starting with the hist tree method. (#10216, #9800, #10311, #9691, #10269, #10251, #10222, #10174, #10080, #10057, #10011, #10138, #10119, #10045, #9876, #9846, #9682) XGBoost now supports launchable inference on SYCL devices, and that work on adding SYCL support for training is ongoing.
Looking ahead, we plan to complete the training in coming releases and then focus on improving test coverage for SYCL, particularly for Python tests.

Optimizations

    • Implement column sampler in CUDA for GPU-based tree methods. This helps us get faster training time when
      column sampling is employed (#9785)    • CMake LTO and CUDA arch (#9677)
    • Small optimization to external memory with a thread pool. This reduces the number of threads launched during
      iteration. (#9605, #10288, #10374) 
Deprecation and breaking changes 
Package-specific breaking changes are outlined in respective sections. Here we list general breaking changes in this release:
    • The command line interface is deprecated due to the increasing complexity of the machine learning ecosystem.
      Building a machine learning model using a command shell is no longer feasible and could mislead newcomers.
      (#9485)
    • Universal binary JSON is now the default format for saving models (#9947, #9958, #9954, #9955). See
      https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/7547(https://github.com/dmlc/xgboost/issues/7547) for more info.
    • The XGBoosterGetModelRaw is now removed after deprecation in 1.6. (#9617)
    • Drop support for loading remote files. This feature lacks any test. Users are encouraged to use dedicated libraries
      to fetch remote content. (#9504)     • Remove the dense libsvm parser plugin. This plugin is never tested or documented (#9799)
    • XGDMatrixSetDenseInfo and XGDMatrixSetUIntInfo are now deprecated. Use the array interface based

       alternatives instead.

Features

This section lists some new features that are general to all language bindings. For package-specific changes, please visit respective sections.
    • Adopt a new XGBoost logo (#10270)
    • Now supports dataframe data format in native XGBoost. This improvement enhances performance and reduces
      memory usage when working with dataframe-based structures such as pandas, arrow, and R dataframe. (#9828,       #9616, #9905)
    • Change default metric for gamma regression to deviance. (#9757)
    • Normalization   for   learning   to   rank   is   now   optional   with   the   introduction   of   the   new
    • Contribution prediction with QuantileDMatrix on CPU. (#10043)
    • XGBoost on macos no longer bundles OpenMP runtime. Users can install the latest runtime from their depen-
      dency manager of choice. (#10440). Along with which, JVM packages on MacoOS are now built with OpenMP 


--- PAGE 493 ---
Bug fixes

    • Fix training with categorical data from external memory. (#10433)
    • Fix compilation with CTK-12. (#10123)
    • Fix inconsistent runtime library on Windows. (#10404)
    • Fix default metric configuration. (#9575)
    • Fix feature names with special characters. (#9923)
    • Fix global configuration for external memory training. (#10173)
    • Disable column sample by node for the exact tree method. (#10083)
    • Fix the FieldEntry constructor specialization syntax error (#9980)
    • Fix pairwise objective with NDCG metric along with custom gain. (#10100)
    • Fix the default value for lambdarank_pair_method. (#10098)
    • Fix UBJSON with boolean values. No existing code is affected by this fix. (#10054)
    • Be more lenient on floating point errors for AUC. This prevents the AUC > 1.0 error. (#10264)
    • Check support status for categorical features. This prevents gblinear from treating categorical features as
      numerical. (#9946)

Document

Here is a list of documentation changes not specific to any XGBoost package.
    • A new coarse map for XGBoost features to assist development. (#10310)
    • New language binding consistency guideline. (#9755, #9866)
    • Fixes, cleanups, small updates (#9501, #9988, #10023, #10013, #10143, #9904, #10179, #9781, #10340, #9658,
      #10182, #9822)
    • Update document for parameters (#9900)
    • Brief introduction to base_score. (#9882)
    • Mention data consistency for categorical features. (#9678)

Python package

Dask

Other than the changes in networking, we have some optimizations and document updates in dask:
    • Filter models on workers instead of clients; this prevents an OOM error on the client machine. (#9518)
    • Users are now encouraged to use from xgboost import dask instead of import xgboost.dask to avoid drawing in
      unnecessary dependencies for non-dask users. (#9742)     • Add seed to demos. (#10009)
    • New document for using dask XGBoost with k8s. (#10271)
    • Workaround potentially unaligned pointer from an empty partition. (#10418)



--- PAGE 494 ---
PySpark

PySpark has several new features along with some small fixes:
    • Support stage-level scheduling for training on various platforms, including yarn/k8s. (#9519, #10209, #9786,
     #9727)
    • Support GPU-based transform methods (#9542)
    • Avoid expensive repartition when appropriate. (#10408)
    • Refactor the logging and the GPU code path (#10077, 9724)
    • Sort workers by task ID. This helps the PySpark interface obtain deterministic results. (#10220)
    • Fix PySpark with verbosity=3. (#10172)
    • Fix spark estimator doc. (#10066)
    • Rework transform for improved code reusing. (#9292)

Breaking changes

For the Python package, eval_metric, early_stopping_rounds, and callbacks from now removed from the fit method in the sklearn interface. They were deprecated in 1.6. Use the parameters with the same name in constructors instead. (#9986)

Features

Following is a list of new features in the Python package:
    • Support sample weight in sklearn custom objective. (#10050)
    • New supported data types, including cudf.pandas (#9602), torch.Tensor (#9971), and more scipy types
      (#9881).
    • Support pandas 2.2 and numpy 2.0. (#10266, #9557, #10252, #10175)
    • Support the latest rapids including rmm. (#10435)
    • Improved data cache option in data iterator. (#10286)
    • Accept numpy generators as random_state (#9743)
    • Support returning base score as intercept in the sklearn interface. (#9486)
    • Support arrow through pandas ext types. This is built on top of the new DataFrame API in XGBoost. See general
      features for more info. (#9612)
    • Handle np integer in model slice and prediction. (#10007)
    • Improved sklearn tags support. (#10230)
    • The base image for building Linux binary wheels is updated to rockylinux8. (#10399)
    • Improved handling for float128. (#10322)

Fixes

    • Fix using categorical data with the score function for the ranker. (#9753)



--- PAGE 495 ---
Document

    • Clarify the effect of enable_categorical (#9877, #9884)
    • Update the Python introduction. (#10033)
    • Fixes. (#10058, #9991, #9573)

Maintenance

    • Use array interface in Python prediction return. (#9855)
    • Synthesize the AMES housing dataset for tests. (#9963)
    • linter, formatting, etc. (#10296, #10014)
    • Tests. (#9962, #10285, #9997, #9943, #9934)

JVM packages

Here is a list of JVM-specific changes. Like the PySpark package, the JVM package also gains stage-level scheduling.

Features and related documents 
    • Support stage-level scheduling (#9775)
    • Allow JVM-Package to access inplace predict method (#9167)
    • Support JDK 17 for test (#9959)
    • Various dependency updates.(#10211, #10210, #10217, #10156, #10070, #9809, #9517, #10235, #10276,
      #9331, #10335, #10309, #10240, #10244, #10260, #9489, #9326, #10294, #10197, #10196, #10193, #10202,       #10191, #10188, #9328, #9311, #9951, #10151, #9827, #9820, #10253)     • Update and fixes for document. (#9752, #10385)
    • Remove rabit checkpoint. (#9599)

Bug Fixes

    • Fixes memory leak in error handling. (#10307)
    • Fixes group col for GPU packages (#10254)

Maintenance

    • Add formatting and linting requirements to the CMake script. (#9653, #9641, #9637, #9728, #9674)
    • Refactors and cleanups (#10085, #10120, #10074, #9645, #9992, #9568, #9731, #9527).
    • Update nvtx. (#10227)
    • Tests. (#9499, #9553, #9737)
    • Throw error for 32-bit architectures (#10005)

      #10133, #10187, #9857, #10042, #10268, #9654, #9835)     • GitHub Action fixes (#10067, #10134, #10064)
